behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert very specific names like 'StdioOrFd', 'Fd(i32)', 'ChildProcessStdio', 'borrow::<ChildProcessStdio>', 'use deno_io::ChildProcessStdio', 'as_i64()', 'libc::dup(', 'from_raw_fd', and the exact removal of 'FileResource' import. The instruction.md never names 'StdioOrFd', 'Fd(i32)', 'StdioOrRid', 'ChildProcessStdio' struct, 'libc::dup', 'from_raw_fd', 'as_i64', or specifies that FileResource import must be removed. These are undiscoverable from instruction.md alone.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly states implementation details: 'The implementation must use a signed integer type for deserialization', 'error messages should reference file descriptor not resource id', 'the runtime to capture its own stdout/stderr handles during initialization', and specifies exact files and what changes are needed in each. This goes well beyond describing what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific names: 'StdioOrFd' enum name (test_stdio_or_fd_enum), 'Fd(i32)' variant (test_fd_variant_with_i32), 'ChildProcessStdio' struct (test_child_process_stdio_struct), exact import 'use deno_io::ChildProcessStdio' (test_file_resource_import_removed), 'libc::dup(' (test_as_stdio_fd_duplication). An alternative correct fix using different names (e.g., 'StdioOrDescriptor', 'OsFd(RawFd)') would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_stdio_or_fd_enum, test_fd_variant_with_i32, test_as_stdio_fd_duplication, etc.) are ALL text-matching tests using regex/string searches on source files. None execute code, spawn processes, or verify runtime behavior. Only the pass-to-pass cargo tests (test_cargo_check_*, test_cargo_test_*) actually execute code.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check source text patterns. An agent could satisfy all f2p tests by writing code with the expected string patterns that doesn't actually work (e.g., dead code containing 'pub enum StdioOrFd', 'Fd(i32)', 'libc::dup(' in comments). The cargo check/clippy tests are pass-to-pass, not fail-to-pass, so they don't gate correctness of the new feature.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/deno adjacent to where the agent works. The agent could read solution/solve.sh (or the patch within it) to extract exact changes. Also, since tests are pure text-matching, the agent could grep test_outputs.py for the exact strings being asserted and write them into the source files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md requires numeric fd passthrough to actually work (child process writes to the fd) and inherit mode with redirected output. No test verifies these runtime behaviors — all f2p tests are text-matching only.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-grouped with section comments (fail-to-pass, pass-to-pass static, pass-to-pass repo_tests), each has a docstring explaining purpose, and naming is descriptive.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output (JSON/CSV/API/DB).

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names exact files (ext/process/lib.rs, ext/io/lib.rs) and describes specific implementation details like 'signed integer type for deserialization', 'ChildStdio struct fields must still be typed with a stdio option type', naming the exact struct. This leaks localization and solution approach.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits/config task.
